programme.title: PBS Washington Week with the Atlantic
programme.desc: PBS Washington Week with The Atlantic is the longest-running primetime news and public affairs program on television. The show features a group of journalists participating in a weekly roundtable discussion of major news events. Moderated by Jeffrey Goldberg, Editor-in-Chief of The Atlantic.

programme.title: Point: Road To Referendum History Bites
programme.desc: A tribute to Neville Bonner, the first Indigenous Australian to enter Federal Parliament.

programme.title: Australian Superbikes
programme.desc: The 2026 Australian Superbike Championship heads to South Australia and to Tailem Bend for round three at The Bend Motorsport Park.

programme.title: Liege-Bastogne-Liege Women's Race
programme.desc: All the highlights from the 2026 Liege-Bastogne-Liege Women's race, on the 156 km route through Belgium.

programme.title: World Gymnastics Rhythmic World Cup
programme.desc: All the best moments from the 2026 World Gymnastics Rhythmic World Cup Series from Baku, Azerbaijan - Part.

programme.title: Plat du Tour
programme.sub-title: Pork Belly With Apples And Cider
programme.desc: Laval, the capital of the Mayenne region, also known as apple country. Today Guillaume meets with Jo-Anne Fahey from Darkes Glenbernie Orchard to try her award winning cider. He then shows us how to easy it is to make his delicious pork belly and apple dish.

programme.title: Cold War Spies
programme.desc: The only thing worse than losing a spy, is losing a spy to the other side. This episode tells the stories of several intelligence operatives who, for a range of reasons, took the high risk high stakes path of betraying their country to spy for the enemy.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Anne Boleyn And Elizabeth I: Prisoner Queens
programme.desc: Historian Tracy Borman reveals how Anne Boleyn shaped the daughter she never knew, and how Elizabeth I became England's most iconic monarch.

programme.title: Bettany Hughes' Lost Worlds: Nabataeans
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes is on a mission to understand the secret behind the success of the Nabataeans. The glorious tombs of their cities like Ancient Hegra in AlUla, Saudi Arabia are proof of a rich kingdom. Following ancient routes from Oman, across the Red Sea, into Greece and Italy, Bettany discovers their ancient monopoly on a prized luxury: incense.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: For decades, experts believed they knew what caused the Hindenburg disaster: that leaking hydrogen ignited - causing the airship to burst into flames. But there may be more to this story. What caused the Hindenburg Disaster?

programme.title: Lost Treasures of Egypt
programme.desc: Archaeologists are on the trail of Tutankhamun. To piece together his story they search his tomb for secrets missed by the first explorers. Near the site of his quarry, they find evidence of a temple he restored to worship the crocodile god, Sobek. And beneath a mysterious fortress in the Eastern Desert, they unearth clues to how he amassed enough gold to fill his tomb with unimaginable treasures.

programme.title: Frogmore House: Royal Retreat
programme.desc: For hundreds of years, the idyllic royal estate of Frogmore has been the private escape for generations of royals. The estate contains Frogmore House, Frogmore Cottage and the Royal Mausoleum. Recently, Frogmore was thrust into the limelight when Harry and Meghan moved into Frogmore Cottage for what turned out to be a short and controversial stay. This program reveals that Frogmore - the Windsors' pleasure palace - has also been the harbourer of dark secrets over the last three centuries.

programme.title: My Rembetika Blues
programme.desc: This documentary film is about love, life, and the soul of human experience. Rembetika or Greek Blues is a music born of exile and the streets. Filmmaker M. Zournazi traces the journey of its roots and how the genre tells the foremost musical accounts of the migrant experience, and urban blues.

programme.title: Matched
programme.sub-title: Virginia And Lopeti + Manpreet And Manpreet
programme.desc: Princess Virginia prepares to marry Lopeti in the grandest Tongan wedding ever to be held in New Zealand but how will Her Royal Highness cope with marriage when she has never cooked or cleaned in her life? And Kiwi-Sikh Manpreet travels back to India for his arranged wedding to a woman with his same name but will they have anything else in common?

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: It's Summer in the Mournes and temperatures are the highest on record. For the mountain vets the heat brings a new set of challenges - including overheated dogs, dehydrated calves and hot badgers.

programme.title: Legacy List With Matt Paxton
programme.desc: A woman about to retire must clear out her entire house before moving to France. Matt helps with the downsize and keeps an eye out for a handful of items she won't leave home without.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.sub-title: Darumbal, Ngaro, Guugu Yimithirr, Tiwi & Bathurst Island Country
programme.desc: A slow TV showcase of the stunning landscapes found in Darumbal, Ngaro, Guugu Yimithirr, Tiwi and Bathurst Island Country.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: euronews
programme.desc: Headlines, Breaking News & Analysis from the Euronews news centre in Brussels, Belgium, in English.

programme.title: PBS News Horizons
programme.desc: Dive into the world of science, health, technology and environmental issues making headlines each week, hosted by PBS News Hour's William Brangham, with in-depth discussions featuring leading experts and professionals.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: PBS Washington Week with the Atlantic
programme.desc: PBS Washington Week with The Atlantic is the longest-running primetime news and public affairs program on television. The show features a group of journalists participating in a weekly roundtable discussion of major news events. Moderated by Jeffrey Goldberg, Editor-in-Chief of The Atlantic.

programme.title: Point: Road To Referendum History Bites
programme.desc: A tribute to Neville Bonner, the first Indigenous Australian to enter Federal Parliament.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: In this episode, Ernie visits Katherine and meets up with a visionary, a landscape photographer, and a river guide who shares stories of the world-famous Nitmiluk Gorge.

programme.title: Matched
programme.desc: Ashleen and Shafee fell in love in their early twenties. Since then, it's been a decade of drama and heartbreak. Former bad boy Shafee has turned his life around and won back his true love but it's not just Ashleen he has to prove himself to. Without Ashleen's mother Nisha's approval, their love story ends, and they will go their separate ways.

programme.title: Hidden Beauty of Northern France
programme.desc: It is a rich region with a stunning coastline and diverse landscapes and wildlife: Northern France, the area between Belgium and the English Channel, has so much to offer.

programme.title: Along Ancient Tracks
programme.desc: Sicily's last narrow-gauge railway has circled the slopes of Mount Etna since the 1930s, connecting isolated villages through landscapes of extraordinary contrast - moon-like lava fields, pistachio plantations, citrus groves, and the dramatic Alcantara Gorge. The line from Adrano to Riposto carries both history and beauty in equal measure.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International SBS Midday
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS News Compass Points
programme.desc: Join PBS News Hour's Nick Schifrin and a panel of experts each week analysing prominent international affairs topics and America's role in a shifting world, breaking down global conflicts, rising tensions, and their implications for U.S. interests.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News Hour
programme.desc: In-depth global news from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Doha, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: For Her, With Her
programme.desc: This documentary charts the course of Women's Legal Service Queensland, from a small but courageous group of young lawyers and social workers banding together in a hostile political climate to an organisation that has helped over 150,000 women and children suffering domestic violence.

programme.title: A Murmuration
programme.desc: A Murmuration speaks to the impact we can have when creativity, community and education intersect. It looks at the power of taking young people's learning into their community, and the healing that comes from connection and sharing our stories.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Extraordinary entertainer Emma Watkins and French food icon Guillaume Brahimi join Adam to release some memorable dinner delights.

programme.title: Lost Treasures of Egypt
programme.desc: Archaeologists investigate the mystery of Tutankhamun's small and poorly decorated tomb. Tutankhamun's reign was shaped by the life of his father, a pharaoh who led the greatest revolution in Egyptian religious history. Was this revolution, and the political intrigue that followed, the cause of Tutankhamun's banishment to a tomb unfit for a Pharaoh?

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed, and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Letters and Numbers
programme.desc: Two contestants pit their linguistic and numerical skills against each other and the clock in this entertaining quiz show. Join host Richard Morecroft along with maths whiz Lily Serna and wordsmith David Astle.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Tonight, the Red Rising trilogy, Labrador Retrievers, the works of John Lennon and the art of Jackson Pollock, in a battle of generations, Labs are guaranteed to stay neutral and adorable.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Reverend Richard Coles joins Robson to explore the Northumberland coastline. They take a Canadian canoe trip along the river Coquet, visit the hidden gem of Walkworth's secret Hermitage and, after a night in the calm of the countryside, make an early-morning crossing to the Holy Island of Lindisfarne where the pair have take part in a photography class and have a go at rope work.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson is joined by Invictus Games Gold medal winner JJ Chalmers. The pair head to Killicrankie where they zip wire across the River Garry before heading to their digs for the night. The following day, the pair take a sled ride through stunning countryside before heading to the Blackruthven Estate where they're given a dry stone walling lesson.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the second of two compilation episodes of 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown. Packed with memorable moments from Jon Richardson, Richard Ayoade, Alan Carr, Danny Dyer, Joe Lycett and Sarah Millican, alongside Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley on numbers.

programme.title: Never Mind the Buzzcocks
programme.sub-title: Perrie, Bradley Simpson, Duncan James, Sam Campbell
programme.desc: Perrie Edwards steps in as captain, teaming up with Blue's Duncan James and Edinburgh Comedy Award winner Sam Campbell. Noel Fielding and Jamali Maddix are joined by The Vamps' Bradley Simpson. The teams tackle absurd boy and girl band trivia and must identify Girl Thang and Irish boyband D-Side in the ID Parade. Plus, there's a special look at Aerosmith's Steven Tyler and his infamously odd feet. Expect laughs, chaos, and plenty of pop nostalgia.

programme.title: SBS World News Late
programme.desc: Reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Sambre: Anatomy of a Crime
programme.desc: 1988. Christine wakes on a roadside path with no clear memory of what has happened to her. When she reports it, the response she receives is far from reassuring. Meanwhile, a young new police recruit is finding his feet, reluctant to question the methods around him. Inside the station, a pattern is beginning to form, but those who could act are choosing to look the other way.

programme.title: Gomorrah
programme.desc: Smart young Camorrista O'Principe rises to power among Ciro's men, his pop star attitude soon provoking a fair amount of envy.

programme.title: Gomorrah
programme.desc: Don Pietro's efforts in stirring disorder and confusion bear fruit, as Ciro's closest aide finds himself in a death trap. But the old don's campaign of vengeance has only just begun.

programme.title: Gomorrah
programme.desc: Ciro is on a mission to keep the members of his alliance in line. However, whatever measures he takes, his men are crazed by envy. And even the 'weaker' sex now smells blood.

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Vet Nigel discovers a worrying growth on much loved cow Florence, but will she have a second chance? Vet Gillian must perform surgery on dog Bailey so that he can be rehomed.

programme.title: Colors of the Islands: The Caribbean
programme.desc: Ep. 1: Waters of Bimini - Master underwater photographer Andre Musgrove is challenged to re-envisage the island of Bimini through his lens. He decided to take the team underwater to show magnificent shipwrecks, wildlife and visuals while photographing master free diver Sophia Palvachinni in these locations.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: This episode of Bamay showcases beautiful Arrernte and Warlpiri Country, with locations such as Mparntwe Alice Springs and the Ellery Creek Big Hole.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: ANC Philippines The World Tonight
programme.desc: Delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: BBC News At Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.

programme.title: Matched
programme.desc: Twenty three-year-old Farhein and celebrity DJ Jilesh are both playing the dating game. The complexity of modern dating isn't the only challenge they have to overcome as they are forced to confront cultural expectations in order to find a lifelong commitment.

programme.title: Hidden Beauty Of Spain
programme.desc: Rough rocks, salt lagoons and fine sandy beaches - Spain's east coast is diverse. The Costa Brava in the far north is fascinating.

programme.title: Van Gogh: The Real Story
programme.desc: Begin by tying together several fascinating facts about Van Gogh's life, which then propel you to ask questions that lead to the uncovering of mysteries: which parts of his life influenced his work?

programme.title: BBC News At Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International SBS Midday
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS Newshour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news, presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.

programme.title: What Does Australia Really Think About
programme.desc: Casey Donovan investigates what Australia really thinks about obesity through emotional personal stories, confronting social experiments, covert filming and an in-depth nationwide survey.
